title: Ostericum grosseserratum
taxonomy: Ostericum grosseserratum is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Apiaceae. It belongs to the genus Ostericum and is native to East Asia. The plant is also known by its synonym, Angelica grosseserrata. Its classification is further supported by its botanical name, which includes the specific epithet "grosseserratum" and the family name "Apiaceae". This classification helps to identify and distinguish Ostericum grosseserratum from other related plant species.
uses: Ostericum grosseserratum is a plant species traditionally used in medicine, particularly in East Asian countries. It is believed to possess anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which contribute to its potential medicinal uses. In traditional medicine, the plant is often used to treat various health conditions, including digestive issues and respiratory problems. The root and rhizome of Ostericum grosseserratum are typically used for medicinal purposes, and are often prepared as teas or infusions. Further research is needed to fully understand the plant's medicinal properties and potential benefits.
benefits: Ostericum grosseserratum is a plant species known for its medicinal properties. It has been traditionally used in Eastern medicine to treat various health issues. constituents: Ostericum grosseserratum contains sesquiterpenoid compounds, including ostericin, which are considered to be the active constituents of the plant. These sesquiterpenoids have been reported to possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. Additionally, Ostericum grosseserratum has been found to contain flavonoids and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The plant's active constituents may also include other terpenoids, such as sesquiterpene lactones. Further research is needed to fully elucidate the composition and effects of Ostericum grosseserratum's active constituents.

intro: Ostericum grosseserratum, also known as Korean angelica tree, is a perennial plant species in the family Apiaceae. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities, and is used to treat various health issues such as fever, rheumatism, and digestive problems. The bioactive constituents of Ostericum grosseserratum include volatile oils, flavonoids, and lignans, which contribute to its therapeutic effects. In traditional herbal medicine, Ostericum grosseserratum is commonly prepared as a decoction or tincture, and is also used in combination with other herbs to make teas, infusions, and topical creams.
